code_state: Phase 2 subagent-delegation-ui published at bdfcc0741. Code at this SHA has delegate type in AtOption, handleAtSelect, groupBy, and rank — but NO group headers, NO pill visual distinction, NO frecency.

coupling.note: No new module couplings. Group header changes stay in slash-popover.tsx. Frecency would add a dependency on Persist (already imported at prompt-input.tsx:50). Two filter pipelines (composer dropdown and @mention) use different exclusion criteria — the plan correctly flags but defers unification.

debuggability.note: UX change, minimal debuggability impact. Frecency tracking could silently fail (never persist). Recommend console.warn on frecency init failure.

convergence.note: Group headers are the right v1 solution for FI-022. Aligned with Phase 2 base. Rank collision (FI-023) mitigation is critical. Frecency adds scope not justified by any follow-up issue.

surface_area.note: AtOption type already exported with delegate variant. PromptPopover props are internal to prompt-input/. No public API changes. Only concern: if visual pill distinction requires AgentPart type expansion (adding description field), that could affect buildRequestParts consumers.

testability.note: No test plan. Popover rendering requires SolidJS DOM. No existing tests for PromptPopover or useFilteredList integration. Group header rendering would be best verified via visual regression. Recommend at minimum: (a) atKey group isolation test, (b) slice-not-splitting-group test.

error_clarity.note: N/A for most changes. Frecency in-memory corruption would be silent. Recommend warning log on load.

reversibility.note: Group headers are pure rendering — revert by removing section labels. Rank change is a single number edit. Frecency adds local storage state (revert needs cleanup). Pill visual distinction touches createPill + pushAgent — revert is clean.

blocking_objections.finding: Group headers require structural changes to PromptPopover. The current flat `For each={props.atFlat.slice(0, 10)}` cannot render section labels between groups. The `slice(0, 10)` must be applied AFTER grouping, or a group header may appear with zero items below it.

Visual distinction between agent and delegate pills (FI-054) is not addressed. createPill sets data-type='agent' for ALL agent selections. handleAtSelect converts both agent and delegate to {type:'agent'} — delegate origin is lost at insertion time. pushAgent cannot distinguish delegates when parsing the DOM.
Rank collision between delegate and agent groups (FI-023). Both use rank 0, causing non-deterministic interleaving depending on insertion order.

conditions_for_approval: C1: Specify the grouped rendering approach in PromptPopover — either pass grouped data from parent or compute group boundaries from the flat list. Verify slice(0,10) does not cut between groups.

C2: Address visual distinction for delegate pills (FI-054) — either carry delegate origin through createPill/pushAgent with data-subagent, or explicitly document in-plan that identical pills are intentional.
C3: Fix rank collision (FI-023) — either merge delegate+agent into one 'people' group (rank 0 with same group key), or assign delegate rank 0.5.
C4: Scope frecency explicitly — either defer to follow-up or specify storage key + cleanup strategy.

follow_up_issues_to_file.title: Unify agent filter pipelines (composer dropdown vs @mention)
follow_up_issues_to_file.description: Composer dropdown excludes mode==='primary'. @mention excludes mode==='subagent'. Different exclusion criteria cause subagent-mode agents to appear in one surface but not the other inconsistently. See prefab-architect-lane8-ux cross-session insight.

follow_up_issues_to_file.title: Delegate description persistence in AgentPart type
follow_up_issues_to_file.description: FI-053: Delegate descriptions shown in popover are lost on commit. If users rely on descriptions to identify which subagent they selected, this is a UX gap. Add optional description to AgentPart type or document as intentional.

follow_up_issues_to_file.title: Frecency for subagent usage ranking
follow_up_issues_to_file.description: Currently no follow-up issue requires frecency. If deemed valuable, create a separate lane with: storage key specification, usage increment hook, sort override in agentList, and cleanup/revert strategy.
